Operator: Good afternoon and welcome to the Nimbus Systems fiscal third quarter 2026 earnings conference call. At this time all participants are in a listen-only mode. Following the prepared remarks we will conduct a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to Priya Raman, Vice President of Investor Relations. Please go ahead.

Priya Raman: Thank you operator and good afternoon everyone. On the call with me today are Daniel Cho, Chief Executive Officer, and Helena Vargas, Chief Financial Officer. Statements made during this call that are not statements of historical fact are forward-looking statements and are subject to the risks and uncertainties discussed in our most recent filings. I will now turn the call over to Daniel.

Daniel Cho: Thanks Priya. Nimbus delivered another solid quarter. Total revenue grew 26 percent year over year to 668 million dollars, with Stratus contributing 452 million, up 31 percent. Net new annual recurring revenue was 64 million dollars in the quarter. The headline event of the quarter was closing the Cirrus Analytics acquisition on schedule in the final week of fiscal Q3, exactly as we committed on the Q2 call. Integration is already underway and we are excited about the long-term opportunity. We continue to see strong consolidation tailwinds across our enterprise customer base.

Helena Vargas: Thanks Daniel. Non-GAAP operating margin for the quarter was 19.2 percent, putting us on track to deliver our full-year fiscal 2026 non-GAAP operating margin at approximately 19 percent, consistent with the updated guidance we provided last quarter. Free cash flow was 118 million dollars. We ended the quarter with 1.4 billion dollars in cash and short-term investments after deploying approximately 540 million dollars for the Cirrus closing consideration. On capital return, we repurchased an additional 110 million dollars of stock in Q3, bringing year-to-date buybacks to 285 million dollars, and we continue to expect to fully exhaust the 500 million dollar authorisation by the end of fiscal 2026. I do however want to flag one downgraded expectation. We previously committed to delivering positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026. Following the closing of Cirrus and the incremental stock-based compensation and amortisation expense it brings, we now expect GAAP operating income to be slightly negative for fiscal 2026, in the range of negative 25 to negative 40 million dollars. We did not achieve that commitment and we want to be transparent about that. We continue to expect GAAP operating profitability in fiscal 2027. With that let us move to questions.

Operator: Our first question comes from Aaron Mitchell of Stifel.

Analyst: Aaron Mitchell from Stifel. Daniel, congratulations on closing Cirrus. Now that the deal is done, can you walk us through the updated integration milestones, specifically the timing for native Cirrus capabilities inside the Stratus console?

CEO: Daniel Cho. Aaron, thank you. With Cirrus now closed, we are reaffirming our prior commitment to deliver native Cirrus query capabilities inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026. Engineering integration is tracking to plan and we will have a customer-facing beta available in early Q4. The general availability target remains end of fiscal Q4 2026.

Operator: Our next question comes from Theo Bennett of Raymond James.

Analyst: Theo Bennett from Raymond James. Daniel, on the Stratus Copilot product. You committed last quarter to general availability by the end of fiscal Q4 2026. The early feedback from your user conference seemed positive. Are you still on track for that GA date?

CEO: Daniel Cho. Theo, I want to be straightforward with you. We are not going to hit the end of fiscal Q4 2026 GA target for Stratus Copilot that we committed to last quarter. Based on feedback we received at the user conference, we are making meaningful architectural changes to the agent runtime so that we can offer stronger enterprise data-isolation guarantees by default. We now expect Stratus Copilot to reach general availability in fiscal Q2 2027 rather than the end of fiscal Q4 2026 timing we previously shared. We considered shipping on the original timeline with a more limited feature set but concluded that the enterprise-readiness work was non-negotiable.

Operator: Our next question comes from Marcia Liu of William Blair.

Analyst: Marcia Liu from William Blair. Helena, can you help us bridge the change in the GAAP operating income outlook? Specifically how much of the swing is one-time deal costs versus ongoing run-rate stock-based compensation from the Cirrus team?

CFO: Helena Vargas. Marcia, of the roughly 65 million dollar swing relative to our prior framework, approximately 25 million is one-time deal and integration cost that will not repeat. The remaining 40 million is ongoing run-rate dilution from Cirrus stock-based compensation and intangible amortisation. The intangible amortisation steps down meaningfully in fiscal 2028 as the acquired technology amortises out. We expect to grow back into GAAP operating profitability during fiscal 2027.

Operator: Our next question comes from Sandra Okonkwo of Needham.

Analyst: Sandra Okonkwo from Needham. On EMEA enterprise. Last quarter you guided EMEA back to mid-single-digit year-over-year growth by fiscal Q4 2026. Has the trajectory held up in Q3?

CFO: Helena Vargas. Sandra, the trajectory has been better than we expected. EMEA net new ARR in Q3 was up 38 percent quarter over quarter and roughly flat year over year, which is a meaningful improvement from the down 5 percent we saw in Q2. We now expect EMEA to return to positive year-over-year growth in Q4 and we are increasingly confident that mid-single-digit growth is achievable.

Operator: Our next question comes from Jens Halvorsen of Barclays.

Analyst: Jens Halvorsen from Barclays. Daniel, beyond Cirrus, can you talk about your inorganic strategy going forward? Should investors expect more M and A in fiscal 2027?

CEO: Daniel Cho. Jens, our default posture is organic. The Cirrus opportunity was unique because of the technology and team fit. We have nothing else of comparable size on the radar. We continue to evaluate small technology tuck-ins to fill specific product gaps, but I would not encourage you to model a meaningful M and A spend for fiscal 2027 at this stage.

Operator: Our next question comes from Yuki Tanaka of Wells Fargo.

Analyst: Yuki Tanaka from Wells Fargo. Helena, you have now spent 285 million dollars of the 500 million dollar buyback authorisation through three quarters. Is the Board considering an expanded authorisation given the cash flow strength?

CFO: Helena Vargas. Yuki, as we said at the start of the year we will revisit the authorisation level at the beginning of fiscal 2027. With the Cirrus capital deployment we want to rebuild a comfortable cash buffer before we commit to an expanded program. We will share our updated capital-return framework on the Q4 call.

Operator: Our next question comes from Wendy Brooks of KeyBanc.

Analyst: Wendy Brooks from KeyBanc. Daniel, last question on competitive dynamics. Are you seeing any change in pricing behaviour from the hyperscaler-native observability tools, and how is your win rate trending?

CEO: Daniel Cho. Wendy, win rates against hyperscaler-native tools held above 75 percent again this quarter in tracked competitive deals. We have seen one of the hyperscalers introduce a more aggressive discounting motion for greenfield workloads, but it has not materially changed our enterprise consolidation deals where customers value the multi-cloud single pane of glass. We will continue to monitor closely.

Operator: That concludes our Q&A session. I would like to turn the call back over to Daniel for closing remarks.

Daniel Cho: Thank you all for joining today. We are executing against our plan, we closed Cirrus on schedule, and we are being transparent about the Stratus Copilot delay and the GAAP operating income outcome. We look forward to seeing many of you on the Q4 call. Thanks again.

Operator: This concludes today's conference call. You may disconnect.
